data.description:
### 2. Incident Overview

* **Scenario Type:** User-Flagged Behavior

#### Expected Behavior

Per sprint SKILL.md Phase 7 Step 0.75 and CLAUDE.md rule 20, the sprint orchestrator MUST dispatch `dso:completion-verifier` at story closure. This applies after any fix that addresses a verifier FAIL — the verifier must be re-run to confirm all DDs now pass before the ticket is closed.

#### Actual Behavior

During epic 5d6e-86a9 sprint, story 270b-0a65 was verified (FAIL on DD5), the gap was fixed (overlay-flag passthrough contract implemented), and the story was closed immediately without re-running the completion verifier. The verifier was skipped because the implementer was confident the fix addressed the gap. The user caught the omission and required a manual re-open → re-verify → re-close cycle.

#### Reproduction

1. Sprint orchestrator runs completion verifier on a story — verifier returns FAIL on one criterion.
2. Orchestrator implements the fix for the failing criterion.
3. Orchestrator closes the story without re-dispatching the verifier.

#### Impact

Story closed with unverified DD status. If the fix had introduced a regression on another DD, it would have been missed. In this case all 6 DDs passed on re-verification, but the protocol was violated.

#### Reference

- CLAUDE.md rule 20: "Never skip dso:completion-verifier dispatch or substitute inline verification"
- Sprint SKILL.md: completion-verifier dispatched at story closure (Step 10a)
- SKILL.md: "Fallback applies ONLY on technical failure (timeout, unparseable JSON), not as permission to skip"
data.title: [Sprint Orchestrator]: Story closed after DD fix without re-running completion-verifier -> DD pass/fail status unverified at closure
